Tag.title: Tag
Tag.description: Represents a keyword or category used to classify legal rulings (fallos) and doctrine articles.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Tag entity.

name.description: The name of the tag (e.g., 'Cláusulas Abusivas', 'Secuestro Prendario').

AdminUser.title: AdminUser
AdminUser.description: Represents an administrative user with permissions to manage content in the application. Authentication is handled externally by Firebase Auth.

id.description: Unique identifier for the AdminUser entity, typically corresponding to a Firebase Auth UID.

email.description: The email address of the administrator.

name.description: The full name of the administrator.

roles.description: A list of roles assigned to the administrator, defining their permissions (e.g., 'contentEditor', 'superAdmin').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the administrator's profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the administrator's profile was last updated.

Fallo.title: Fallo
Fallo.description: Represents a judicial ruling (fallo) related to savings plans, displayed on the platform.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Fallo entity.

title.description: The title of the judicial ruling.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the fallo, used in dynamic pages (e.g., '/fallos/[slug]').

summary.description: A brief summary of the judicial ruling.

content.description: The full text or detailed content of the judicial ruling.

tribunal.description: The court or tribunal that issued the ruling.

date.description: The date when the judicial ruling was issued.

tagIds.description: References to Tags associated with this ruling for categorization. (Relationship: Tag N:N Fallo)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this fallo record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this fallo record was last updated.

published.description: Indicates whether the fallo is publicly visible on the website.

Doctrina.title: Doctrina
Doctrina.description: Represents a legal doctrine article or blog post, providing expert analysis and information.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Doctrina entity.

title.description: The title of the doctrine article.

slug.description: A URL-friendly identifier for the article, used in dynamic pages (e.g., '/doctrina/[slug]').

summary.description: A brief introductory summary of the doctrine article.

content.description: The full body content of the doctrine article.

authorId.description: Reference to the AdminUser who authored this article. (Relationship: AdminUser 1:N Doctrina)

publishDate.description: The date when the doctrine article was published.

tagIds.description: References to Tags associated with this article for categorization. (Relationship: Tag N:N Doctrina)

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this doctrine article record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this doctrine article record was last updated.

published.description: Indicates whether the doctrine article is publicly visible on the website.

FAQ.title: FAQ
FAQ.description: Represents a frequently asked question and its answer, optimized for SEO.

id.description: Unique identifier for the FAQ entity.

question.description: The text of the frequently asked question.

answer.description: The detailed answer to the frequently asked question.

order.description: An integer representing the display order of the FAQ item.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this FAQ record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this FAQ record was last updated.

published.description: Indicates whether the FAQ item is publicly visible on the website.

LegalProblemSection.title: LegalProblemSection
LegalProblemSection.description: Represents a dedicated section/page detailing a specific legal problem related to savings plans, optimized for SEO.

id.description: Unique identifier for the LegalProblemSection entity.

slug.description: The URL-friendly slug for the problem section (e.g., 'liquidacion', 'rescision').

title.description: The main title of the legal problem section page.

explanation.description: The clear and detailed explanation of the legal problem.

seoTitle.description: The SEO-optimized title for the page's metadata.

seoDescription.description: The SEO-optimized description for the page's metadata.

ctaBlockContent.description: Content for the Call to Action block within this section, prompting users to consult their case.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when this legal problem section record was created in the system.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when this legal problem section record was last updated.

published.description: Indicates whether the legal problem section is publicly visible on the website.

CaseEvaluation.title: CaseEvaluation
CaseEvaluation.description: Represents a user's case evaluation submitted through the AI chat assistant.

id.description: Unique identifier for the CaseEvaluation entity.

clientName.description: The full name of the client submitting the case (from AI output 'nombre').

city.description: The city where the client resides (from AI output 'ciudad').

province.description: The province where the client resides (from AI output 'provincia').

adminCompany.description: The name of the administering company for the savings plan (from AI output 'administradora').

planStatus.description: The current status of the savings plan (from AI output 'estadoPlan').

adjudicated.description: Indicates if the savings plan has been adjudicated (from AI output 'adjudicado').

vehicleReceived.description: Indicates if the vehicle related to the plan has been received (from AI output 'vehiculoRecibido').

groupOrder.description: The group and order number of the plan, if known (from AI output 'grupoOrden').

mainProblemExplanation.description: The client's free-text explanation of the main problem (from AI output 'problemaPrincipal').

factsSummary.description: An AI-generated summary of the case facts based on the chat conversation (from AI output 'resumenHechos').

availableDocumentation.description: A list of documentation the client indicates they have (from AI output 'documentacionDisponible'). E.g., ['contrato', 'liquidación', 'recibos'].

urgencyLevel.description: The detected urgency level of the case (from AI output 'urgencia').

urgencyReason.description: The reason identified for the urgency level (from AI output 'motivoUrgencia').

possibleLegalCategory.description: An AI-suggested main legal category for the case (from AI output 'posibleCategoriaJuridica').

suggestedNextAction.description: An AI-suggested next action or step based on the case summary (from AI output 'proximaAccionSugerida').

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the case evaluation was submitted.

status.description: The current status of the case evaluation within the system.

chatTranscript.description: An optional transcript of the entire chat conversation leading to the case evaluation. Useful for auditing and context.

definition.description: Stores administrative user profiles and their assigned roles. Document ID is the Firebase Auth UID. Used for Database Access Control (DBAC) roles.

params.name: adminUserId
params.description: The unique Firebase Auth UID of the administrative user.

definition.description: Categorization labels for Fallos and Doctrina. Publicly readable, only modifiable by administrative users.

params.name: tagId
params.description: The unique identifier for the tag.

definition.description: Judicial rulings related to savings plans. Publicly readable if 'published' is true. Modifiable only by administrative users.

params.name: falloId
params.description: The unique identifier for the judicial ruling.

definition.description: Legal doctrine articles. Publicly readable if 'published' is true. Modifiable only by administrative users. Includes 'authorId' for specific author permissions.

params.name: doctrinaId
params.description: The unique identifier for the doctrine article.

definition.description: Frequently asked questions and their answers. Publicly readable if 'published' is true. Modifiable only by administrative users.

params.name: faqId
params.description: The unique identifier for the FAQ item.

definition.description: Dedicated pages detailing specific legal problems. Publicly readable if 'published' is true. Modifiable only by administrative users.

params.name: sectionId
params.description: The unique identifier for the legal problem section.

definition.description: User case submissions from the AI chat assistant. Created anonymously by any user. Only readable and updatable by administrative users. Includes 'status' for workflow management.

params.name: caseEvaluationId
params.description: The unique identifier for the case evaluation submission.

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure prioritizes clarity, security, and scalability by adhering to the core design principles, especially Authorization Independence and Structural Segregation.

**1. Authorization Independence:**
*   **Content Collections (Fallos, Doctrina, FAQs, LegalProblemSections):** Authorization for public visibility is achieved by including a `published: boolean` field directly within each document. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls to parent collections or external documents to determine read access for public users. For example, a rule can simply be `allow read: if resource.data.published == true;`.
*   **Doctrina Authoring:** The `Doctrina` entity includes an `authorId` field, allowing rules to directly check `request.auth.uid == resource.data.authorId` for author-specific permissions without requiring additional `get()` operations on other collections for that specific document's authorization.
*   **Case Evaluations:** Each `CaseEvaluation` document is entirely self-contained, meaning all data relevant for its processing and status is within the document itself. No authorization dependencies exist on other collections for its creation or subsequent access by administrators.
*   **Global Role Checks (AdminUser):** While the `isAdmin` helper function (used for `read` and `write` access across most content and `CaseEvaluation` collections) involves a `get()` call to the `/admin_users/{adminUserId}` collection, this is a controlled exception. This `get()` is for retrieving global user metadata (roles) tied to the `request.auth.uid` itself, not for establishing hierarchical authorization dependencies between application data entities. It directly implements the DBAC principle by storing roles in the database and associating them with the user's UID.

**2. Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
*   **Public vs. Admin Access:** Content entities like `/fallos`, `/doctrina`, `/faqs`, and `/legal_problem_sections` are stored in top-level collections. Their `published` status internally segregates visibility (public vs. admin-only for drafts/unpublished). This keeps content relevant to a single entity type together while providing distinct access based on a direct document field.
*   **Admin User Data:** The `/admin_users` collection is dedicated solely to administrator profiles, ensuring that user authentication and role management data are separated from application-specific content.
*   **Case Evaluations:** The `/case_evaluations` collection is entirely segregated. It's designed for anonymous creation and admin-only reading/updating, ensuring that sensitive case information is never publicly exposed and has a distinct security profile.

**3. Querying with Authorization Predicates (QAPs):**
*   **Public Content Listings:** For collections like `/fallos` or `/doctrina`, a client can perform a secure `list` query (e.g., `db.collection('fallos').where('published', '==', true).get()`). The Firestore security rules enforce `allow list: if resource.data.published == true;`, guaranteeing that only published documents are returned, and the rules are not acting as filters that could lead to incomplete result sets or insecure data exposure.
*   **Admin-only Listings:** For the `/case_evaluations` collection, `list` operations are secure for administrators because the rule `allow list: if isAdmin(request.auth.uid);` ensures that only authenticated and authorized administrators can retrieve the documents. This is a clear QAP as it applies to the entire collection and its access is binary based on the requesting user's role.

By implementing these strategies, the Firestore structure provides a clear, debuggable, and scalable foundation for the application's security and data management.
